opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is covered in these weeping little blisters that itch so intensely it feels like burning. It came up a couple of days after I started wearing a new belt with a metal buckle, and it's driving me mad — I couldn't sleep for the itch.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The itch is unbearable and now some of the blisters have burst and are oozing, and I'm worried it could be something serious like shingles or a reaction.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 48 hours ago, a day or two after I started wearing a new belt with a metal buckle pressed against my skin
symptom_narrative.duration: two days and it changed as it went along
symptom_narrative.character: it started as a mild burning feeling, then over the next day turned into red bumps and tiny blisters exactly where the buckle sits; in the last 12 hours some of the blisters popped and started weeping clear fluid, and now bits of it are drying and crusting over
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is really severe, like a burning itch — worst thing is it kept me awake
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: just on my belly, right where the belt buckle presses, in a neat rectangle shape — nowhere else
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant itching, worse when I'm warm or when clothing rubs it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: a bit of dabbed-on calamine lotion helped for a few minutes but that's all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching, heat, and my waistband rubbing on it
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no hives anywhere else, no trouble breathing, no swelling of my face or lips — I feel well in myself apart from the itch
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before that I can remember; maybe cheap watch-backs and jewellery have irritated my skin in the past
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: lost a night's sleep and I keep getting distracted trying not to scratch it through my shirt

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: calamine lotion I bought from the chemist

allergies_as_reported.substance: cheap metal jewellery / watch backs (skin gets irritated)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; independent; quite distressed and sleep-deprived because of the relentless itch

objective_data_offered.value: a sharply outlined rectangular red patch, roughly matchbox-sized, on the skin below the navel exactly where the belt buckle sits, studded with tiny blisters — some burst and weeping clear fluid, some crusting; the skin all around it looks completely normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: shown on a phone camera; lighting and focus limit how well fine detail can be judged

objective_data_offered.value: the new belt with the shiny metal buckle is here — I can hold it up to the camera
I checked with a thermometer and I don't have a temperature
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported single reading; thermometer not validated
